prompt: A FieldTrace Rescue & Supply Chain management system that includes offline account access, evidence capture, standardized item data, traceability publishing controls, controlled facility check-ins, and analytics for day-to-day shelter and warehouse operations. Staff users sign in with a local username and password on a Leptos web UI and are assigned roles of Administrator, Operations Staff, and Auditor. The home workspace shows today's intake and transfer queue, pending inspections, and exceptions. Users maintain their profile, privacy preferences, and a local address book for shipping destinations (US format with ZIP+4 validation and phone masking), and can request account deletion with a 7-day cooling-off period. Operations Staff can capture photos, short videos (up to 60 seconds), or audio notes (up to 2 minutes) directly from the device; the UI automatically stamps a visible watermark containing facility code and local timestamp (MM/DD/YYYY hh:mm AM/PM). Evidence can be linked to an intake record, inspection result, traceability code, or check-in event, with default retention of 365 days and manual legal-hold override. Product and supply entries support guided parsing for item name, SKU, size, color, price and discount in USD, stock status, media references, and short review summaries, then present a standardized view with unit conversion (oz/lb, in/ft). The check-in ledger supports scanning a barcode or manual entry of a member ID; anti-passback prevents re-entry within 2 minutes unless an Administrator overrides with a reason. A dashboard provides filters by region, time range, status, and tags, plus full-text search and CSV export. The backend uses Axum to expose local REST-style APIs consumed by the Leptos frontend, with SQLite as the single-node persistence layer so the entire system remains fully functional without internet access. Passwords must be at least 12 characters with a stored salted hash; sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity. Traceability codes are generated locally with a checksum and can be verified offline. Sensitive fields such as phone numbers and addresses are encrypted at rest with a locally managed key.
